title: PDF Ambush Kills 2 Junta Troops in Setottara Township, Sagaing — Final Day of Thingyan
detail: On April 16, 2026 — the final day of Myanmar's Thingyan Water Festival — resistance forces in Setottara Township (Sagaing Region) ambushed a Myanmar junta military patrol, killing two SAC soldiers and wounding one. Setottara Township (Sit Tike) sits in northern Sagaing Region, where PDF units have conducted sustained guerrilla operations against SAC patrols and supply columns throughout 2025–2026. The attack demonstrates that resistance forces in Sagaing continued active operations even as the SAC conducted its own Thingyan-period air campaign — both sides refusing a holiday ceasefire. The Thingyan period (April 13–16, 2026) saw the SAC conduct 110+ aerial attacks in Ayadaw Township alone while the PDF maintained ground operations across Sagaing and Magway. The event was documented as part of daily conflict reporting by local CDM and resistance networks.

media.caption: PDF ambush kills 2 SAC soldiers in Setottara Township, Sagaing, on the final day of Thingyan 2026

title: Ayadaw Township (Sagaing): SAC Conducts 110+ Aerial Attacks During Entire Thingyan Festival Period
detail: Ayadaw Township in central Sagaing Region was subjected to over 110 aerial attacks by Myanmar military aircraft across the Thingyan Water Festival period (April 13–17, 2026), including on April 16. Ayadaw Township sits on critical PDF supply and movement corridors in central Sagaing; the SAC has subjected it to one of the highest concentrations of aerial attacks of any single township. The 110+ attack count during a 5-day holiday period represents approximately 22 attacks per day — a figure far exceeding what even the SAC's own propaganda would justify as counter-insurgency. These operations include fixed-wing jet strikes, helicopter gunship operations, and gyrocopter low-altitude attacks on villages and farmland. The pace of aerial operations during Thingyan was among the highest recorded in recent months, directly contradicting the SAC's image management around the Buddhist New Year period. Many Ayadaw residents had preemptively fled to jungle shelters before the festival began.

media.caption: Over 110 aerial attacks on Ayadaw Township, Sagaing Region, during Myanmar's Thingyan Water Festival 2026

title: Junta Forces Torture and Kill 17-Year-Old Detainee in Yesagyo Township — Body Dumped in River
detail: A 17-year-old detained by Myanmar junta forces in Yesagyo Township, Magway Region, was tortured and killed between April 14–16, 2026; the victim's body was subsequently dumped in a river. The case is consistent with the SAC's documented pattern of extrajudicial executions, particularly of young people suspected of supporting the resistance. Yesagyo Township in Magway Region has seen increased resistance activity as PDF units extend operations southward from Sagaing. Junta forces have conducted sweeps arresting young civilians as suspected PDF sympathizers, often using them as intelligence sources through torture before executing them. Disposing of bodies in rivers — used to prevent proper burial and obstruct documentation — mirrors dozens of cases documented by Fortify Rights, AAPP, and Human Rights Watch across Sagaing and Magway regions. AAPP documents over 2,000 deaths in SAC detention — a figure widely considered a severe undercount, as many families are never notified and bodies are never returned.

media.caption: 17-year-old tortured and killed in SAC detention in Yesagyo Township, Magway; body dumped in river
